skill.name: performance-testing-review-multi-agent-review
skill.description: Guides multi-agent code review planning, routing, synthesis, and validation for performance-aware software assessments.
skill.summary: Coordinate specialized review agents for broader code quality, security, architecture, and performance feedback.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in markdown prose and illustrative Python snippets. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, destructive commands, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks flagged as command execution
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are inline markdown references, the literal $ARGUMENTS placeholder, or fenced Python examples. They do not execute Ruby, shell commands, or user-controlled subprocesses.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown code fences and documentation examples, not runnable skill code. No script file, subprocess call, or command execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptography pattern in ordinary text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in descriptive text and example labels. No hash functions, encryption APIs, password handling, or cryptographic decisions are implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines contain metadata, prose, or workflow phase names. There is no cryptographic code path to assess.

low_findings.title: False positive: Reconnaissance pattern in section headings
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are headings or orchestration labels for hybrid review strategies. They do not instruct the agent to inventory the host, collect system data, or probe networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text describes review workflow structure. No evidence of host reconnaissance, network scanning, or environment enumeration was found.

content.value_statement: Complex code reviews often miss issues when one reviewer covers every concern. This skill helps route work across specialized review agents and synthesize prioritized results.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to use a multi-agent code review workflow.
Lists specialized reviewer roles for security, architecture, performance, compliance, and quality.
Shows routing logic for selecting agents based on project context.
Explains parallel, sequential, and hybrid review execution patterns.
Describes synthesis, conflict resolution, and quality validation steps.
Provides example review scenarios for web applications and microservices.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not run review agents by itself.
Example functions are illustrative and are not complete implementations.
It references an optional playbook file that is not present in this package.
Review quality depends on the available project context and reviewer prompts.

use_cases.title: Plan a release review
use_cases.description: Coordinate security, performance, architecture, and deployment readiness checks before a major release.

use_cases.title: Review performance-sensitive changes
use_cases.description: Route code changes to performance and architecture reviewers while preserving security and quality coverage.

use_cases.title: Standardize review reports
use_cases.description: Combine findings from multiple reviewers into one prioritized report with conflicts resolved clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Basic review plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-agent review plan for this codebase: [project or files]. Include the reviewer roles and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Performance-focused review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes with performance, security, and architecture agents. Prioritize risks that could affect production latency or scalability.

prompt_templates.title: Hybrid execution workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a hybrid multi-agent review workflow for [system]. Specify parallel phases, sequential dependencies, conflict resolution, and validation criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize agent findings
prompt_templates.prompt: Merge these agent findings into one review report. Deduplicate issues, resolve conflicts, assign severity, and list verification steps.

output_examples.input: Review a web service before release.
output_examples.output: A review plan with security, architecture, performance, and deployment reviewers, plus a prioritized report structure.

output_examples.input: Combine conflicting agent feedback about caching.
output_examples.output: A synthesized recommendation that explains tradeoffs, confidence, owner, severity, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Create a performance review workflow for a microservice.
output_examples.output: A staged workflow covering bottleneck analysis, dependency review, regression checks, and deployment readiness.

best_practices: Give each reviewer a clear scope, input set, and expected output.
Run independent reviews in parallel, then synthesize results in one final pass.
Record confidence, severity, evidence, owner, and verification steps for every finding.
anti_patterns: Do not ask every agent to perform the same broad review.
Do not merge conflicting findings without explaining the decision.
Do not treat illustrative examples as working orchestration code.

faq.question: Does this skill execute review agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance, routing examples, and synthesis practices for manual or agent-assisted reviews.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can it review performance test results?
faq.answer: It can help structure a performance-focused review, but it does not parse or run test results by itself.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no confirmed malicious behavior, executable commands, network access, or prompt injection attempts.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide target files, repository context, review goals, performance constraints, and any known release risks.

faq.question: What is the main limitation?
faq.answer: The skill is a conceptual workflow guide. Teams must implement any real agent orchestration separately.
